#SourceHeadline (condensed)SignalDirection
1Digitimes 🇹🇼 CoWoS-L fully booked H2 2026 — NVIDIA GB300 ramp, 26-week lead time CoWoS price raise 🟡 MIXED — demand confirmed, zero buffer

INTC Inverse Signal: Taiwan Strait escalation is the only kill condition in CivilisationOS that is bullish for one stock and bearish for all others. Intel Foundry Services benefits from customer fab diversification demand. Channel formula: strait_score = (1.0 − raw_strait_score) for INTC only.
Q3 2026 Outlook — Three-Month Signal Calendar
Three conditions that sustain the positive case:
🟢 No PLA major exercise (≥20 aircraft single ADIZ incursion)
🟢 N2 yield holds (above ~65%)
🟢 TWD stable (above 30.0 TWD/USD)
All three green → Q3 positive bias holds.

Supply Chain Risk Intelligence
Chokepoint · Freight Rate · Critical Minerals · Nearshoring · Logistics
SCRI  0.3900  WATCH

TSMC / Taiwan Canary operates an advanced, deeply integrated supply chain network with deep logistics infrastructure, multiple port redundancies, and sophisticated inventory management — reducing but not eliminating chokepoint sensitivity. Maritime chokepoint exposure is concentrated across: Taiwan Strait (critical dependency, weight 0.95); Strait of Malacca (high dependency, weight 0.65).

Critical import dependencies include crude oil, natural gas, semiconductor materials, food commodities. Energy import dependency means commodity price spikes and Middle East shipping disruptions transmit directly to domestic inflation and current account pressure.

Primary exports — semiconductors, electronics, petrochemicals, machinery — are sensitive to global freight rate movements and trade route disruptions.

SCRI 0.3900 WATCH — Structural supply chain sensitivity is elevated. Chokepoint and critical import dependencies warrant close monitoring. A single trigger event (Strait closure, export ban, major port strike) could escalate to ELEVATED within days.

Watch signals: Taiwan Strait closure or capacity restriction → immediate freight rate spike  |  Middle East escalation → energy import disruption and current account deterioration  |  SCFI/WCI/BDI freight index spike above 2-standard-deviation threshold → freight stress trigger

Kill Condition Breakdown
Kill Condition Weight Score Zone

KC_SC02 Freight Rate Stress25%0.0800NORMAL
KC_SC03 Critical Minerals20%0.3200WATCH

Real Estate Risk Intelligence
Property Price · Mortgage Stress · Commercial RE · Developer Debt · REIT
RERI  0.1000  NORMAL

TSMC / Taiwan Canary is classified as a developed real estate market. Primary stress vector is residential affordability. Commercial real estate exposure is limited CRE concentration risk.

As a developed market, Taiwan property values are sensitive to interest rate cycles — the aggressive central bank tightening of 2022-2024 has left mortgage holders with elevated refinancing risk. Commercial office markets face structural headwinds from remote work adoption globally.

No active developer stress flagged. Property developers in Taiwan are not currently assessed as in distress at a systemic level.

RERI 0.1000 NORMAL — Property market stable at structural baseline. No systemic stress indicators active.

Watch signals: Mortgage refinancing cliff (2025-2027 vintage) → household stress spike  |  Property price index 3-month decline exceeding 2% → KC_RE01 trigger

Insurance Risk Intelligence
Natural Catastrophe · Reinsurance · Property Withdrawal · Financial Guaranty · Climate
INRI  0.3200  WATCH

TSMC / Taiwan Canary faces 3 identified primary natural catastrophe perils: Earthquake, Hurricane / Typhoon, Flood. High-severity events (Earthquake, Hurricane / Typhoon) are present — these carry the highest economic loss potential and are most likely to cause reinsurance market dislocations.

The insurance market in Taiwan is moderately developed, with adequate domestic capacity but reinsurance-dependent for tail events. Reinsurance profile: high reinsurance dependency — most catastrophic risk ceded offshore.

Climate risk classification: Tier 2 — moderate climate vulnerability with known transition risks. Transition risks (carbon pricing, stranded assets in fossil energy) and moderate physical risks are on the radar. Insurer pricing adjustments are likely within the 3-5 year horizon.

INRI 0.3200 WATCH — Insurance market faces structural vulnerabilities. Nat-cat exposure and/or reinsurance dependency requires monitoring. A major catastrophic event could expose coverage gaps rapidly.

Watch signals: Major Earthquake event → test of reinsurance capacity and coverage adequacy  |  Global reinsurance market hardening (Jan or July renewals) → premium spike or capacity withdrawal  |  Cat bond spread widening → forward signal of reinsurance market stress

Kill Condition Breakdown
Kill Condition Weight Score Zone

KC_IN03 Property Ins. Withdrawal20%0.1500NORMAL
KC_IN04 Financial Guaranty12%0.0500NORMAL
KC_IN05 Climate Risk Dislocation8%0.1400NORMAL

The insurance market in Taiwan is moderately developed, with adequate domestic capacity but reinsurance-dependent for tail events. Reinsurance profile: high reinsurance dependency — most catastrophic risk ceded offshore.

Climate risk classification: Tier 2 — moderate climate vulnerability with known transition risks. Transition risks (carbon pricing, stranded assets in fossil energy) and moderate physical risks are on the radar. Insurer pricing adjustments are likely within the 3-5 year horizon.

INRI 0.3200 WATCH — Insurance market faces structural vulnerabilities. Nat-cat exposure and/or reinsurance dependency requires monitoring. A major catastrophic event could expose coverage gaps rapidly.

Watch signals: Major Earthquake event → test of reinsurance capacity and coverage adequacy  |  Global reinsurance market hardening (Jan or July renewals) → premium spike or capacity withdrawal  |  Cat bond spread widening → forward signal of reinsurance market stress

Kill Condition Breakdown
Kill Condition Weight Score Zone
KC_IN01 Natural Catastrophe35%0.3000WATCH
KC_IN02 Reinsurance Market25%0.2000NORMAL
KC_IN03 Property Ins. Withdrawal20%0.1500NORMAL
KC_IN04 Financial Guaranty12%0.0500NORMAL

Supply Chain Risk Intelligence
Chokepoint · Freight Rate · Critical Minerals · Nearshoring · Logistics
SCRI  0.3900  WATCH

TSMC / Taiwan Canary operates an advanced, deeply integrated supply chain network with deep logistics infrastructure, multiple port redundancies, and sophisticated inventory management — reducing but not eliminating chokepoint sensitivity. Maritime chokepoint exposure is concentrated across: Taiwan Strait (critical dependency, weight 0.95); Strait of Malacca (high dependency, weight 0.65).

Critical import dependencies include crude oil, natural gas, semiconductor materials, food commodities. Energy import dependency means commodity price spikes and Middle East shipping disruptions transmit directly to domestic inflation and current account pressure.

Primary exports — semiconductors, electronics, petrochemicals, machinery — are sensitive to global freight rate movements and trade route disruptions.

SCRI 0.3900 WATCH — Structural supply chain sensitivity is elevated. Chokepoint and critical import dependencies warrant close monitoring. A single trigger event (Strait closure, export ban, major port strike) could escalate to ELEVATED within days.

Watch signals: Taiwan Strait closure or capacity restriction → immediate freight rate spike  |  Middle East escalation → energy import disruption and current account deterioration  |  SCFI/WCI/BDI freight index spike above 2-standard-deviation threshold → freight stress trigger

Kill Condition Breakdown
Kill Condition Weight Score Zone

KC_SC02 Freight Rate Stress25%0.0800NORMAL
KC_SC03 Critical Minerals20%0.3200WATCH

Insurance Risk Intelligence
Natural Catastrophe · Reinsurance · Property Withdrawal · Financial Guaranty · Climate
INRI  0.3200  WATCH

TSMC / Taiwan Canary faces 3 identified primary natural catastrophe perils: Earthquake, Hurricane / Typhoon, Flood. High-severity events (Earthquake, Hurricane / Typhoon) are present — these carry the highest economic loss potential and are most likely to cause reinsurance market dislocations.

The insurance market in Taiwan is moderately developed, with adequate domestic capacity but reinsurance-dependent for tail events. Reinsurance profile: high reinsurance dependency — most catastrophic risk ceded offshore.

Climate risk classification: Tier 2 — moderate climate vulnerability with known transition risks. Transition risks (carbon pricing, stranded assets in fossil energy) and moderate physical risks are on the radar. Insurer pricing adjustments are likely within the 3-5 year horizon.

INRI 0.3200 WATCH — Insurance market faces structural vulnerabilities. Nat-cat exposure and/or reinsurance dependency requires monitoring. A major catastrophic event could expose coverage gaps rapidly.

Watch signals: Major Earthquake event → test of reinsurance capacity and coverage adequacy  |  Global reinsurance market hardening (Jan or July renewals) → premium spike or capacity withdrawal  |  Cat bond spread widening → forward signal of reinsurance market stress

Supply Chain Risk Intelligence
Chokepoint · Freight Rate · Critical Minerals · Nearshoring · Logistics
SCRI  0.3900  WATCH

TSMC / Taiwan Canary operates an advanced, deeply integrated supply chain network with deep logistics infrastructure, multiple port redundancies, and sophisticated inventory management — reducing but not eliminating chokepoint sensitivity. Maritime chokepoint exposure is concentrated across: Taiwan Strait (critical dependency, weight 0.95); Strait of Malacca (high dependency, weight 0.65).

Critical import dependencies include crude oil, natural gas, semiconductor materials, food commodities. Energy import dependency means commodity price spikes and Middle East shipping disruptions transmit directly to domestic inflation and current account pressure.

Primary exports — semiconductors, electronics, petrochemicals, machinery — are sensitive to global freight rate movements and trade route disruptions.

SCRI 0.3900 WATCH — Structural supply chain sensitivity is elevated. Chokepoint and critical import dependencies warrant close monitoring. A single trigger event (Strait closure, export ban, major port strike) could escalate to ELEVATED within days.

Watch signals: Taiwan Strait closure or capacity restriction → immediate freight rate spike  |  Middle East escalation → energy import disruption and current account deterioration  |  SCFI/WCI/BDI freight index spike above 2-standard-deviation threshold → freight stress trigger

Real Estate Risk Intelligence
Property Price · Mortgage Stress · Commercial RE · Developer Debt · REIT
RERI  0.1000  NORMAL

TSMC / Taiwan Canary is classified as a developed real estate market. Primary stress vector is residential affordability. Commercial real estate exposure is limited CRE concentration risk.

As a developed market, Taiwan property values are sensitive to interest rate cycles — the aggressive central bank tightening of 2022-2024 has left mortgage holders with elevated refinancing risk. Commercial office markets face structural headwinds from remote work adoption globally.

No active developer stress flagged. Property developers in Taiwan are not currently assessed as in distress at a systemic level.

RERI 0.1000 NORMAL — Property market stable at structural baseline. No systemic stress indicators active.

Watch signals: Mortgage refinancing cliff (2025-2027 vintage) → household stress spike  |  Property price index 3-month decline exceeding 2% → KC_RE01 trigger
